notes: Official Origin product capture for TreasuryBench V1.
response:
Your total spending for May 2026 reached $5,502.72, which represents a 31% increase compared to your spending in April. While your expenses were higher this month, your income of $13,960 resulted in a substantial monthly surplus of $8,457.28—a fantastic savings rate of approximately 60.60% for the month. 🎉
May spending breakdown
Your spending was concentrated across a few key areas, with household costs and professional travel making up more than half of your total monthly outflow.
Household: $2,100.00 (38.20% of total)
This consists of your rent payment to Eastside Flats.
Business travel: $1,115.08 (20.30% of total)
Your client onsite trip to Atlanta included charges from Marriott Marquis ($612.30), Delta Air Lines ($428.60), and Uber ($74.18).
Healthcare: $575.00 (10.40% of total)
This includes your HealthCare.gov marketplace premium of $415 and a $160 visit to Austin Therapy Group.
Groceries: $365.47 (6.60% of total)
Spending was split between Costco ($238.62) and H-E-B ($126.85).
Business rent: $350.00 (6.40% of total)
Your monthly membership at WeWork Austin.
HSA contribution: $300.00 (5.50% of total)
Your contribution to your Fidelity HSA.
Software: $210.83 (3.80% of total)
This covers multiple professional tools including Adobe Creative Cloud, Amazon Web Services, Webflow, Google Workspace, and Figma.
The remaining $386.34 was distributed among office supplies ($186.40), drinks & dining ($184.95), internet ($79.99), and your phone bill ($35.00).
Insights and observations
Your professional expenses—including business travel, business rent, office supplies, and software—totaled $1,862.31 this month. Given that you received significant client payments from Stripe, Notion, and Figma totaling $13,960, these expenses appear to be well-supported by your freelance income. 📈
It is also great to see that you are prioritizing your future health costs by contributing $300 to your HSA alongside your standard insurance premiums. This disciplined approach to both your business and personal finances is positioning you well for long-term stability.
